tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria), with additional findings of ketonuria, glucosuria, and low urine specific gravity. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). HbA1c is low, suggesting possible anemia or altered red cell turnover. Findings are consistent with renal dysfunction, possible nephrotic syndrome, and metabolic derangements; clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
